module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Successful login with valid credentials
description: Verify a registered user can log in successfully with correct username/email and password.
expectedResult: User is authenticated and redirected to the application's home/dashboard page; a valid session/token is created.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Login fails with incorrect password
description: Verify the system rejects login attempts with a valid username but wrong password.
expectedResult: Login is rejected with a generic error message (e.g., 'Invalid username or password'); user remains on the login page; no session is created.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Login fails with non-existent username
description: Verify the system rejects login attempts for a username/email that does not exist in the system.
steps: Enter a username/email not registered in the system
expectedResult: Login is rejected with a generic error message that does not reveal whether the account exists (prevents user enumeration).

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Empty username and password submission
description: Verify client-side validation prevents submission when both fields are empty.
steps: Leave both username and password fields blank
expectedResult: Form does not submit; inline validation messages such as 'Username is required' and 'Password is required' are displayed.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Empty password with valid username
description: Verify validation triggers when password field is left blank.
expectedResult: Form does not submit (or is rejected server-side); 'Password is required' validation message is shown.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Empty username with valid password
description: Verify validation triggers when username field is left blank.
expectedResult: Form does not submit; 'Username is required' validation message is shown.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Username/email field case sensitivity
description: Verify whether login succeeds when username/email is entered with different letter casing.
steps: Enter a valid username/email but with mixed/upper case (e.g., User@Example.com vs user@example.com)
expectedResult: Login succeeds if usernames are intended to be case-insensitive (typical for emails); behavior should match documented spec.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Password field is masked
description: Verify password input is masked by default and not visible in plain text.
steps: Click into the password field and type a password
expectedResult: Characters are displayed as dots/asterisks; password is not shown in plain text unless a 'show password' toggle is explicitly used.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Show/hide password toggle (if present)
description: Verify the show/hide password icon, if present, correctly toggles password visibility.
steps: Enter a password into the password field
expectedResult: Password text toggles between masked and plain text correctly without altering the entered value.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Invalid email format validation
description: Verify the username/email field validates proper email format if email is used as the identifier.
expectedResult: Inline validation error is shown indicating invalid email format; form is not submitted to the server.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: SQL injection attempt in login fields
description: Verify the login form is not vulnerable to SQL injection via username/password fields.
steps: Enter a SQL injection payload in the username field (e.g., admin' OR '1'='1)
expectedResult: Login is rejected as invalid credentials; no database error is exposed; no unauthorized access is granted.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: XSS injection attempt in login fields
description: Verify the login form sanitizes/escapes script input and is not vulnerable to reflected XSS.
steps: Enter a script payload (e.g., <script>alert(1)</script>) into username and/or password fields
expectedResult: Input is treated as plain text/escaped; no script executes; error message displayed without rendering injected HTML/JS.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Account lockout after repeated failed attempts
description: Verify brute-force protection locks or throttles the account after a defined number of consecutive failed login attempts.
steps: Enter a valid username with an incorrect password
Repeat the failed attempt the configured threshold number of times (e.g., 5 times)
Attempt to log in again, including with the correct password
expectedResult: Account is temporarily locked or a CAPTCHA/delay is triggered; further attempts (even with correct credentials) are blocked until lockout period expires or account is unlocked.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Login with leading/trailing whitespace in credentials
description: Verify the system trims or correctly handles whitespace entered before/after username or password.
steps: Enter a valid username with leading/trailing spaces (e.g., '  user@example.com  ')
expectedResult: Username is trimmed appropriately and login succeeds; password whitespace handling matches the security spec (typically not trimmed).

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Maximum length input boundary test
description: Verify the system handles extremely long input strings in username/password fields gracefully.
steps: Enter an extremely long string (e.g., 5000+ characters) into the username field
Enter an extremely long string into the password field
expectedResult: Input is either truncated/rejected with a clear validation message, or handled gracefully without server error, crash, or exposed stack trace.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: 'Forgot Password' link navigation
description: Verify the 'Forgot Password' link (if present) navigates to the correct password reset flow.
expectedResult: User is redirected to a password reset page where they can request a reset link via email/username.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: 'Remember Me' functionality (if present)
description: Verify the 'Remember Me' checkbox persists the session/login across browser restarts as expected.
steps: Enter valid credentials and check 'Remember Me'
Close and reopen the browser, then revisit the application URL
expectedResult: User remains logged in without needing to re-enter credentials, per the 'Remember Me' duration policy.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Login without 'Remember Me' checked
description: Verify session does not persist after browser closure when 'Remember Me' is unchecked.
steps: Enter valid credentials without checking 'Remember Me'
Close and reopen the browser, then revisit the application URL
expectedResult: User is prompted to log in again; session was not persisted.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Session expiry / token timeout
description: Verify the user's session expires correctly after the configured inactivity/token timeout period.
steps: Leave the session idle for longer than the configured timeout period
Attempt to perform an action or navigate within the app
expectedResult: User is logged out automatically and redirected to the login page with a session-expired message.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Direct access to authenticated page without login
description: Verify unauthenticated users are redirected to the login page when attempting to access protected routes directly.
steps: Without logging in, attempt to navigate directly to a known authenticated/dashboard URL within the app
expectedResult: User is redirected to https://flexos.test.varshealth.com/login (or equivalent) instead of viewing protected content.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Back button after logout does not restore session
description: Verify browser back navigation after logout does not expose previously authenticated pages from cache.
steps: Log in successfully and navigate to a few authenticated pages
expectedResult: User is redirected to the login page or shown an unauthenticated state; cached authenticated content/data is not accessible.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Logout functionality
description: Verify the logout action correctly terminates the session and invalidates the token.
expectedResult: User is redirected to the login page; session/token is invalidated server-side; protected pages are no longer accessible.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Concurrent login from multiple devices/browsers
description: Verify behavior when the same user logs in simultaneously from two different sessions/devices.
steps: Log in as the same user on Browser A
Log in as the same user on Browser B
expectedResult: Behavior matches the application's policy — either both sessions remain active concurrently, or the earlier session is invalidated, per documented requirements.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: HTTPS enforcement and secure cookie flags
description: Verify the login page and authentication tokens are transmitted securely.
steps: Inspect the page URL and confirm it loads over HTTPS
Attempt to access via HTTP if applicable
Inspect cookies/tokens set after login using browser dev tools
expectedResult: Page is served only over HTTPS (HTTP redirects to HTTPS); authentication cookies are flagged Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite as appropriate.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: CSRF protection on login form
description: Verify the login request includes anti-CSRF protections where applicable.
steps: Inspect the login request/response for CSRF token usage (via dev tools/network tab)
Attempt to replay/submit the login form from an unauthorized origin
expectedResult: Login form includes CSRF protections (token, SameSite cookies, or equivalent); cross-origin submission is rejected.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Disabled/locked account login attempt
description: Verify a user with a disabled or administratively locked account cannot log in.
steps: Enter credentials for an account known to be disabled/locked
expectedResult: Login is rejected with a clear message (e.g., 'Account disabled, contact administrator') without exposing sensitive account state details beyond what's intended.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Login with special characters and Unicode in password
description: Verify the password field correctly accepts and validates special characters and Unicode (e.g., emoji, accented letters).
steps: Enter a password containing special characters/Unicode that matches a registered account's actual password
expectedResult: Login succeeds when the credentials match exactly, confirming no encoding/normalization issues corrupt the password.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Tab order and keyboard accessibility
description: Verify the login form is fully operable via keyboard alone, including submission with Enter key.
steps: Navigate to the login page using only the keyboard (Tab key)
Tab through username, password, and 'Login' button in logical order
Enter valid credentials and press 'Enter' to submit
expectedResult: Focus order is logical; all fields/buttons are reachable and operable via keyboard; pressing Enter submits the form successfully.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Screen reader / ARIA label accessibility
description: Verify form fields and error messages are properly labeled for assistive technologies.
steps: Inspect the login form's HTML/ARIA attributes using browser dev tools or an accessibility checker
Trigger a validation error and inspect how it is announced
expectedResult: Input fields have associated labels/aria-labels; error messages are programmatically associated with their fields (e.g., aria-describedby) for screen reader announcement.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Responsive layout on mobile and tablet viewports
description: Verify the login page renders correctly and remains usable across различных screen sizes.
steps: Open the login page on a mobile viewport (e.g., 375x667)
Open the login page on a tablet viewport (e.g., 768x1024)
Verify form elements, branding, and buttons render without overlap or cutoff
expectedResult: Login form is fully visible, correctly laid out, and usable across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewport sizes.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Cross-browser compatibility
description: Verify the login page functions consistently across major supported browsers.
steps: Open the login page in Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari
Perform a successful login in each browser
expectedResult: Login page renders consistently and login succeeds in all supported browsers without functional differences.

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Page load performance
description: Verify the login page loads within an acceptable time threshold.
steps: Measure page load time using browser dev tools (Network tab) or a performance testing tool
expectedResult: Page fully loads (DOM interactive and form usable) within the agreed performance SLA (e.g., under 3 seconds on standard network conditions).

module: generate the testcases for logi auth
title: Error message does not reveal sensitive system details
description: Verify error responses do not leak stack traces, server details, or internal implementation information.
steps: Trigger various login failure scenarios (wrong password, server error simulation if possible)
Inspect the displayed error message and network response payload
expectedResult: Error messages are generic and user-friendly; no stack traces, internal paths, or sensitive debug data are exposed in the UI or API response.
